nodes.title: Publish a verified pure Common Lisp kernel laboratory
nodes.outcome: A public v0.1.0-alpha repository lets a Common Lisp developer verify a pure reference/candidate pair under fresh SBCL semantic and measured profiles, run the experimental RQ8 and mirror-compiler examples, and inspect bounded machine-readable evidence without receiving local, secret, binary, or third-party workspace artifacts.
successCriteria: The standalone verifier passes every positive and adversarial integration test under SBCL 2.6.6 or newer.
The lawful exact-integer e-graph passes its complete bounded unit suite.
The rational-quant and mirror-kernel ASDF systems load and pass deterministic tests from any absolute checkout path.
README, Apache-2.0 license, notices, contribution policy, CI, and claim boundaries are present and internally consistent.
A release scan finds no credentials, generated keys, binaries, toolchains, personal absolute paths, or unrelated artifacts.
The verified commit history is pushed to a public GitHub repository and tagged v0.1.0-alpha with release notes.
Hosted CI runs the unchanged release gates on hash-pinned SBCL 2.6.6 and succeeds on the public main branch.
constraints: Call the output generated typed Common Lisp compiled by SBCL, never a new SBCL implementation.
Keep reference implementations unchanged beside every optimized candidate.
Never use safety 0 or claim portability for SBCL-specific measured evidence.
Keep every performance claim bound to its workload, runtime, machine, and baseline.
Do not copy toolchains, binaries, TLS keys, PDFs, DOCX files, local reports, or third-party source checkouts.

nodes.title: Establish the release repository contract
nodes.input: A new empty Git repository and the accepted one-repository alpha architecture.
nodes.action: Create ignore rules, stable agent instructions, a normalized roadmap, explicit non-overlapping scope contracts, machine-readable handoff and changelog records, validate them, and commit only the foundation.
nodes.output: A conflict-safe release repository with one machine-readable task source and explicit security boundaries.
verification: Parse every .agents JSON file.
Validate and query .agents/roadmap.json.
Run the durable dispatch-wave validator.
Confirm ignored secret, binary, cache, and toolchain patterns.
Confirm the foundation commit contains only foundation files.
evidence: All 11 coordination and scope JSON documents parsed successfully.
The roadmap validator returned valid=true with 7 nodes, 13 edges, and step-000 as the sole active next step before closure.
The dispatch-wave validator returned valid=true with no conflicts or unscoped active work.
Ignore probes matched generated keys, certificates, binaries, toolchains, Python caches, agent logs, and the frozen local input staging tree.
The three immutable staged inputs are bound by file counts and deterministic tree SHA-256 values in .agents/input-manifest.json.
Git diff whitespace validation returned zero errors.

nodes.title: Make the SBCL kernel verifier standalone
nodes.input: The locally verified compile-pure-lisp skill sources, contracts, and tests.
nodes.action: Import the verifier, pipeline compiler, bounded lawful e-graph compiler, contracts, CLI documentation, and complete positive and negative tests without local path dependencies.
nodes.output: A standalone evidence-driven SBCL kernel verification tool and optional Codex skill wrapper.
verification: Run all verifier integration tests.
Run all lawful e-graph unit tests.
Verify a positive pipeline case and a positive e-graph case with fresh semantic and measured profiles.
Confirm mismatch, mutation, compiler-warning, and output-collision cases fail closed.
evidence: The standalone verifier integration suite passed 8 of 8 tests under isolated SBCL 2.6.6, including positive pipeline and e-graph cases.
The lawful exact-integer e-graph unit suite passed all 13 checks under isolated SBCL 2.6.6.
Benchmark mismatch and candidate input mutation were rejected during the semantic profile before any measured profile or timing.
Compiler-warning, compiler-load-warning, unrelated-redefinition-warning, and derived-output collision cases all failed closed.
The Codex skill wrapper passed the skill-creator quick validator with its YAML dependency supplied from a disposable validation directory.
Core worker, pipeline compiler, and lawful e-graph compiler files are byte-identical to the frozen accepted inputs; release files contain no personal absolute paths or key markers.

nodes.title: Package rational-quant as a tested experiment
nodes.input: The RQ8 ASDF package with its reference and guarded SBCL kernels.
nodes.action: Import the package, add explicit author/license/test metadata, document the numeric and storage contract, and add deterministic shape, boundary, fallback, bit-equivalence, storage, and nonmutation tests.
nodes.output: A self-contained experimental RQ8 package with honest storage and performance boundaries.
verification: Run rational-quant ASDF tests under SBCL.
Check finite-domain, unsupported-domain, exact-scale, realistic-scale, storage, and nonmutation cases.
Compile at safety 3 with zero warning conditions.
Confirm no claim generalizes beyond the declared fixtures.
evidence: The direct RQ8 gate passed 62 deterministic checks under isolated SBCL 2.6.6 with the compiler safety minimum restricted to 3.
The checkout-independent ASDF test-op passed the same 62 checks after fresh compilation into a temporary output tree.
Both gates rejected escaped warning or serious-condition diagnostics; SBCL emitted informational compiler notes only.

Fixtures cover exact power-of-two scales, zero and multi-group inputs, nonfinite and subnormal rejection, bit-exact reference equality, fallback behavior, storage accounting, and input/tensor/vector nonmutation.
Package and core Lisp sources contain no safety-0 form and remain byte-identical to the frozen accepted inputs.
Documentation explicitly limits the package to SBCL and makes no performance claim.

nodes.title: Package the bounded mirror compiler experiment
nodes.input: The tested 24-node mirror-kernel prototype and the accepted rational-quant package.
nodes.action: Import the mirror compiler, preserve its exact-cover and fail-closed contracts, make testing checkout-path independent, and record only bit-exact and no-regression claims supported by fresh evidence.
nodes.output: A bounded symbolic-to-typed-SBCL compiler experiment with deterministic tests and narrow evidence.
verification: Run mirror-kernel ASDF tests from an arbitrary absolute checkout path.
Run fresh verifier correctness profiles for the generated kernel.
Confirm unsupported shapes fail closed or use the documented public fallback.
Reject general-compiler and material-speedup claims.
evidence: Direct and checkout-independent ASDF gates each passed 37 deterministic checks under isolated SBCL 2.6.6 with safety restricted to 3.
The semantic graph contained exactly 24 nodes and the accepted target covered each node exactly once in dependency order.

Disabling the ordered-fold pattern returned an unsupported result with no partial entries or schedule; unsupported strict runtime shapes signaled an error while the public wrapper preserved the documented fallback.
The standalone verifier accepted 12 deterministic cases in both fresh semantic and measured correctness profiles with no compiler warnings.
The verifier was required only to establish correctness; no timing, allocation, performance-equivalence, portability, whole-system, or material-speedup claim was retained.
Compiler and generated RQ8 wrapper sources remain byte-identical to the frozen accepted inputs, and all generated build/report artifacts stayed outside the checkout.

nodes.title: Harden and verify the alpha release
nodes.input: The accepted standalone verifier, rational-quant package, and mirror-kernel experiment.
nodes.action: Add public documentation, Apache-2.0 licensing and provenance, contribution/security guidance, portable test runners, CI, sanitized evidence summaries, and run the complete release gate plus path, secret, binary, and provenance scans.
nodes.output: A locally committed v0.1.0-alpha candidate safe for public distribution.
verification: Run python3 scripts/test-all.py successfully.
Parse every tracked JSON document and validate/query the roadmap.
Run whitespace, mode, absolute-path, secret, key, binary, and generated-artifact scans over the exact tracked set.
Confirm README claims match fresh reports and declared negative boundaries.
evidence: The one-command release gate passed roadmap validation, 8 verifier integration tests, 13 lawful e-graph checks, both 62-check RQ8 modes, and the complete mirror gate under SBCL 2.6.6.

The release-content audit passed over 63 tracked or unignored candidate files and parsed 16 JSON documents with zero path, secret, key, binary, generated-artifact, whitespace, license, or claim-boundary errors.
Apache-2.0 license text, notice, contribution rules, trusted-code security boundary, CI, release notes, and evidence documentation are present.
The reasoned claim record proves only the 2048-value RQ8 payload saving, classifies bounded mirror equivalence as empirically supported, and classifies whole-system superiority as unknown.
Portfolio analysis retained one compile-pure-lisp skill and kept RQ8 and mirror-kernel as examples rather than creating overlapping skills.
All runtime/build outputs remained in disposable directories; the publishable tree contains no personal absolute path or bundled third-party source/toolchain.

nodes.title: Publish the verified alpha
nodes.input: A clean locally committed alpha candidate, authenticated GitHub CLI, and the exact tracked release set.
nodes.action: Create a public GitHub repository, push the scoped history, create annotated tag v0.1.0-alpha, publish bounded release notes, and verify the remote refs and release metadata.
nodes.output: A public, reproducible, explicitly experimental Pure Lisp Kernel Lab alpha release.
verification: Verify origin points to the intended public repository.
Verify remote main equals the local accepted commit.
Verify tag v0.1.0-alpha resolves to the accepted commit.
Verify the published release notes preserve all negative claim boundaries.
evidence: GitHub CLI authentication was active for bibi-chat with SSH protocol, and SSH authentication returned GitHub's success banner after the live ED25519 host fingerprint matched GitHub's authenticated metadata.
The target did not previously exist; GitHub created bibi-chat/pure-lisp-kernel-lab as PUBLIC with origin git@github.com:bibi-chat/pure-lisp-kernel-lab.git.
The complete SBCL 2.6.6 release gate passed all six suites and the release audit passed 63 candidate files plus 16 JSON documents with zero findings immediately before publication.
Remote main and the peeled annotated v0.1.0-alpha tag resolve to the final step-050 commit.
The GitHub v0.1.0-alpha prerelease targets the verified tag and publishes docs/RELEASE_NOTES.md with the no-whole-system-speed and SBCL-specific boundaries intact.

nodes.title: Make hosted CI reproduce the accepted runtime
nodes.input: A public alpha whose workflow installs Ubuntu SBCL 2.2.9 and fails the mirror measured profile while the complete local gate passes on the declared SBCL 2.6.6 runtime.
nodes.action: Replace the unpinned distro runtime with a cached build from the hash-verified official SBCL 2.6.6 source archive, keep the semantic and audit commands unchanged, and verify the hosted run on the exact pushed commit.
nodes.output: Public GitHub Actions evidence that the complete release gate succeeds on the same declared SBCL version used for local acceptance.
verification: Validate the workflow and confirm the source URL, version, and SHA-256 are explicit.
Run the complete local SBCL 2.6.6 gate and release audit without changing kernel code or claim boundaries.
Push one scoped step commit and verify its GitHub Actions run completes successfully on SBCL 2.6.6.
evidence: The workflow pins official SBCL 2.6.6 source URL and SHA-256 a65a7a30812aaf54925d1192b9b9e810f527c79911c6000b7548105aef7da34b, matching the previously accepted toolchain provenance.
The unchanged complete local gate passed all six suites on SBCL 2.6.6 and the release audit passed 64 candidate files plus 17 JSON documents with zero findings.

GitHub Actions run 29668603547 completed successfully on implementation commit 5191e112e272010ae88d102dd3bc0f847f57e141; its log identified SBCL 2.6.6, passed all six gates, and repeated the 64-file/17-JSON zero-error audit.
No kernel, verifier, package, benchmark, release-note, or performance-claim source changed in the hosted-runtime repair.
